start.speaker: elara
start.text: You're back. Good — I was hoping you would be.

options.text: Any new developments with the water?
I heard something about the well. What do you know?

ally_update.speaker: elara
ally_update.text: I've been running more tests on the water samples. The nightcap root concentration is increasing — someone is adding more. This isn't a one-time poisoning. It's ongoing.

ally_update_2.speaker: elara
ally_update_2.text: But there's something else. I saw a figure near the well two nights ago. Hooded, moving carefully. They were carrying something — a jar or a vial. By the time I got close, they were gone.

options.text: We should stake out the well. Catch them in the act.
Could you tell anything about them? Height, build, clothing?
A hooded figure with vials... sounds like a certain priest.

stakeout_plan.speaker: elara
stakeout_plan.text: I was thinking the same. Tomorrow night, I'll wait in the herb garden — it overlooks the well. If you can position yourself on the other side, by the old oak, we'll have them surrounded. Meet me after sunset.

figure_description.speaker: elara
figure_description.text: Tall. Thin. Moved like someone used to sneaking around. The cloak was dark — not the brown wool the villagers wear. Something finer. And their hands... I thought I saw something on their fingers. Ash, maybe. Or soot.

options.text: Ash on their hands. The Ashen Light uses ash in rituals, don't they?
That could be anyone. We need more evidence.

maren_suspicion.speaker: elara
maren_suspicion.text: I've thought the same since he arrived. But suspicion isn't proof, and he's got half the village eating from his hand now. If we accuse him publicly without evidence, WE become the villains. We need to be careful.

tips.speaker: elara
tips.text: A few things that might help your investigation: the chapel has a cellar that Brother Maren keeps locked. Greta at the inn hears everything. And Old Maren — the druid, not the priest — she can feel things through the earth that we can't see.

disappointed_but_sharing.speaker: elara
disappointed_but_sharing.text: Worse. Two more families lost their last stores. Children are going hungry. I've been stretching my herb supplies thin trying to keep people from getting sick on the bad water.

disappointed_but_sharing_2.speaker: elara
disappointed_but_sharing_2.text: I won't pretend I'm not disappointed you wouldn't help before. But I'll tell you this much — I saw someone by the well at night. Someone who shouldn't have been there. This village is being poisoned, and whoever is doing it is still at it.

grateful_update.speaker: elara
grateful_update.text: Your gold kept three families fed this week. They don't know who helped them — I thought it best to keep it quiet. But I wanted you to know it mattered.

grateful_update_2.speaker: elara
grateful_update_2.text: I've been watching the well at night. Two nights ago, I saw a hooded figure — tall, thin, ash on their hands — adding something to the water. The poisoning is deliberate and ongoing.

well_sighting.speaker: elara
well_sighting.text: You've heard? I saw someone at the well two nights ago. A figure in a dark cloak, pouring something into the water. I tried to follow but they vanished into the shadows near the chapel.

options.text: I want to help. Tell me what you need.
I'll keep digging. Stay safe, Elara.
I need to go. We'll talk again.

late_acceptance.speaker: elara
late_acceptance.text: Thank you. I know I can't do this alone. Check the well at night — and be careful around the chapel. Whoever is doing this knows what they're doing.

farewell.speaker: elara
farewell.text: You too. And... thank you. For coming back. It means more than you know.
